name: Commit and push all existing work
prompt: Git add all files, commit with message 'feat: core memoreei MCP server — storage, search, connectors, tools', and push to origin main. Do NOT add .env or memoreei.db or __pycache__ or .venv to git. Check .gitignore covers them.
test: git log --oneline -1 | grep -q 'feat: core' && git status --porcelain | grep -v '^\.env$\|memoreei\.db\|__pycache__\|TASK\.md\|PROGRESS\.md\|PLAN\.md' | wc -l | grep -q '^0$'

name: Verify MCP server starts and responds
prompt: Test that the MCP server starts correctly. Run: echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"0.1.0"}}}' | timeout 10 .venv/bin/python -m memoreei.server — and verify it returns a valid JSON-RPC response with server capabilities listing the tools. If there are errors, fix them. The server must start cleanly via stdio transport.
test: echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"0.1.0"}}}' | timeout 10 .venv/bin/python -m memoreei.server 2>/dev/null | head -1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert 'result' in d, 'no result'; print('server ok')"

name: Seed sample data and verify search works end-to-end
prompt: Run the seed script to populate the database with WhatsApp sample data: .venv/bin/python scripts/seed_data.py. Then write a quick test script that does an end-to-end search: connect to the database, run a hybrid search for 'sentient printer', and verify it returns results from the printer conspiracy chat. If seed_data.py or the search has errors, fix them. The embedding model will need to download on first run — that's expected.
test: .venv/bin/python -c "import asyncio; from memoreei.storage.database import Database; from memoreei.search.hybrid import HybridSearch; from memoreei.search.embeddings import get_provider; db=Database('./memoreei.db'); loop=asyncio.get_event_loop(); loop.run_until_complete(db.connect()); e=get_provider(); hs=HybridSearch(db,e); results=loop.run_until_complete(hs.search('sentient printer',limit=5)); assert len(results)>0, 'no results'; print(f'search ok: {len(results)} results')"

name: Test Discord connector with real channel
prompt: Test the Discord connector against the real channel. The .env file has DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID=REDACTED_CHANNEL_ID. Write a quick test: use the discord connector to fetch messages from the channel. If the channel is empty that's fine — just verify it connects, authenticates, and returns without error. Fix any issues. The connector should use Discord HTTP API (not gateway/websocket).
test: .venv/bin/python -c "import asyncio,os; from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(); from memoreei.connectors.discord_connector import DiscordConnector; dc=DiscordConnector(os.environ['DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN']); result=asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(dc.fetch_messages(os.environ['DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID'],limit=10)); print(f'discord ok: {len(result)} messages')"

name: Test each MCP tool via JSON-RPC
prompt: Write a script at scripts/test_mcp_tools.py that tests each MCP tool by sending JSON-RPC requests to the server via stdio. It should: 1) Start the server as a subprocess, 2) Send initialize, 3) Call tools/list and verify all 6 tools are listed, 4) Call search_memory with query='printer', 5) Call list_sources, 6) Call add_memory with some test content. Print results for each. Make it runnable and make sure it passes.

name: Create Claude Code MCP configuration
prompt: Create a .mcp.json file in the project root that configures Memoreei as an MCP server for Claude Code. The config should point to the server via: {"mcpServers":{"memoreei":{"command":".venv/bin/python","args":["-m","memoreei.server"],"env":{"MEMOREEI_DB_PATH":"./memoreei.db"}}}}. Also verify the config is valid JSON.
test: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.mcp.json')); assert 'mcpServers' in d; assert 'memoreei' in d['mcpServers']; print('config ok')"

name: Write README.md
prompt: Write a professional README.md for the Memoreei project. Include: 1) Project name + emoji + one-line description, 2) What it does (3 sentences), 3) ASCII architecture diagram showing connectors -> ingestion -> storage -> MCP server -> clients, 4) Quick start section (clone, install, seed data, configure Claude Code, run), 5) MCP tools documentation (all 6 tools with params), 6) Supported data sources, 7) Configuration (.env options), 8) Privacy note (local-first, data stays on your machine), 9) License: MIT. Keep it clean and hackathon-demo worthy.
test: python3 -c "content=open('README.md').read(); assert len(content)>2000, 'too short'; assert 'search_memory' in content; assert 'MIT' in content; assert 'Quick' in content.lower() or 'start' in content.lower(); print('readme ok')"

name: Final commit and push
prompt: Stage all changes, commit with message 'feat: MCP tools tested, Claude Code config, README — hackathon ready', and push to origin main. Verify push succeeds.
test: git diff --cached --quiet && git push origin main 2>&1 | grep -q 'Everything up-to-date\|main -> main'
